entries.description: Rolling 24-bar 5-minute close-return volatility.
entries.hypothesis: High short-horizon realized volatility should reduce long-only selection quality after costs and execution constraints.

entries.description: Rolling 12-bar 5-minute close-return volatility.
entries.hypothesis: A medium intraday volatility penalty may complement the 24-bar risk filter with less regime lag.

entries.description: Rolling 6-bar 5-minute close-return volatility.
entries.hypothesis: A short intraday volatility penalty may catch fast deterioration before longer windows react.

entries.description: Absolute close-to-close return scaled by turnover.
entries.hypothesis: Price impact can capture intraday crowding or liquidity stress that is not captured by raw volume or turnover ratios.

entries.description: Current turnover divided by the 48-bar rolling average minus one.
entries.hypothesis: Persistent turnover expansion can indicate participation and tradability, but must be evaluated against cost and crowding.

research_memory.negative_findings: The 2026-05-20 legacy revalidation kept the factor on watchlist. Full and high-cost returns were positive, but admission remained watchlist and the factor belongs in horizon or policy review rather than the confirmed core.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retest only with stricter turnover controls or entry/exit buffers, and require positive incremental contribution after correlation and contribution caps.

entries.description: Current turnover standardized by a 48-bar rolling mean and standard deviation.
entries.hypothesis: Extreme turnover shocks may be informative, but the latest admission report marks the standalone signal as economically weak after costs.

research_memory.negative_findings: Standard admission kept the factor on watchlist because standalone economics were weak after transaction-cost and stability gates; do not reuse as a primary alpha without a targeted redesign.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only as a conditional crowding or liquidity-stress overlay, or after demonstrating positive portfolio contribution net of costs in annual slices.

entries.description: Current volume divided by the 48-bar rolling average minus one.
entries.hypothesis: Volume expansion may help separate tradable attention from volatility-only effects, but current standalone economics are weak.

research_memory.negative_findings: Volume expansion was statistically usable but did not clear the economic or stability bar in standard admission; raw volume ratio should not be reintroduced as a standalone long signal.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only with price-confirmation, liquidity-adjusted transforms, or a regime filter that proves incremental portfolio contribution after costs.

entries.description: Twelve-bar close-to-close return, currently used in inverted direction when reviewed.
entries.hypothesis: Short intraday continuation may reverse after execution lag and costs; keep as a controlled watchlist signal before reuse.

research_memory.negative_findings: Short intraday momentum required inverted treatment and remained on watchlist because execution lag and annual stability were not strong enough for default promotion.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only as a reversal or overreaction variant with explicit holding-period and cost controls, not as raw short-horizon continuation.

entries.description: Close price position within the rolling 48-bar intraday high-low range, centered around zero.
entries.hypothesis: A close near the top of the recent range may indicate stronger short-horizon demand than a close near the bottom of the range.

research_memory.negative_findings: Rejected in the 2026-05-15 batch: rank IC was positive but directional hit rate and stable-year count were insufficient, so range position did not show reliable standalone selection value.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only after redesigning as an overextension reversal or breakout-confirmation feature with materially different transform and documented stability improvement.

entries.description: Rolling 48-bar high-low range volatility based on log intrabar range.
entries.hypothesis: High high-low range volatility should signal unstable execution conditions and lower long-only selection quality after costs.

entries.description: Directional close-to-close move divided by the total absolute 1-bar path length over the same window.
entries.hypothesis: Very efficient intraday directional moves may be overextended after execution lag; the current admission report supports the inverted direction.

entries.description: Close-to-VWAP stretch relative to a rolling 48-bar intraday VWAP.
entries.hypothesis: Large positive stretches above rolling VWAP should be prone to short-horizon mean reversion after entry lag and trading costs.

research_memory.negative_findings: Rejected in the 2026-05-15 batch: standalone IC and spread were not stable enough across years, and the feature failed hit-rate/stability gates despite being point-in-time safe.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only with a different microstructure hypothesis such as volume-weighted mean reversion, signed deviation buckets, or liquidity-conditioned recovery; do not rerun the same raw deviation.

entries.description: Rolling 48-bar downside close-return semivolatility using only negative 5-minute returns.
entries.hypothesis: Downside-only realized volatility should be a cleaner long-only risk penalty than symmetric volatility because recent selling pressure worsens both selection quality and execution risk.
entries.notes: Formal 2023-2025 admission cleared all gates in inverted direction. The equal two-factor candidate portfolio was negative and the isolated alpha sleeve lost 34.96%, so do not promote it as standalone alpha. A lagged downside-volatility gross-exposure gate improved the promoted decorrelated policy from 32.21% to 38.77% full-window return, improved high-cost return from 20.31% to 27.31%, reduced drawdown, and passed all standard validation checks.

entries.description: Rolling 48-bar skewness of 5-minute close-to-close returns.
entries.hypothesis: Intraday return skewness can separate unstable lottery-like bursts from smoother accumulation; the direction should be learned or gated because positive skew may represent either attention chasing or genuine demand.

research_memory.negative_findings: The 2023-2025 standard admission showed strong inverted rank IC, but the cost-adjusted top-minus-bottom spread was negative, so the factor is not economically admissible as a standalone signal.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only as a tail-risk filter or after adding liquidity and turnover controls that turn annual cost-adjusted spread positive.

entries.description: Rolling 48-bar Chaikin-style money-flow balance from close location within each 5-minute high-low range, weighted by volume.
entries.hypothesis: Closes near intrabar highs on sustained volume should proxy net accumulation more directly than raw volume expansion and may improve attention signals after costs.

research_memory.negative_findings: The 48-bar money-flow factor was rejected in standard admission: coverage was below the hard threshold, directional hit rate was only 0.507, and only one annual slice had stable IC direction.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only with a materially different accumulation transform, such as volume-decayed close-location value or liquidity-conditioned money flow, and require full coverage plus stable annual direction.

entries.description: Rolling 48-bar signed turnover imbalance, using the sign of each 5-minute close-to-close return to classify turnover as up-pressure or down-pressure.
entries.hypothesis: Turnover attached to upticks should be more informative than raw turnover shocks because it combines participation with directional pressure while remaining computable from standard bars.

research_memory.negative_findings: The signed turnover imbalance only worked in inverted direction and failed the cost-adjusted spread gate despite passing basic IC and coverage checks.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only after separating up-pressure and down-pressure legs or adding liquidity participation constraints that demonstrate positive cost-adjusted spread.

entries.description: Rolling 48-bar close momentum divided by realized 5-minute return volatility over the same window.
entries.hypothesis: Volatility-scaled intraday continuation may separate durable demand from noisy price jumps, reducing overlap with raw momentum signals that were unstable after costs.

research_memory.negative_findings: Risk-adjusted momentum required inverted treatment, had only two stable annual IC slices, and failed the cost-adjusted spread gate in the standard 2023-2025 admission run.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only as a reversal/overextension signal with explicit holding-period and liquidity controls, not as raw volatility-scaled continuation.

entries.description: Rolling 48-bar close momentum multiplied by current volume expansion versus the same-window rolling mean.
entries.hypothesis: Continuation confirmed by contemporaneous volume expansion should be more robust than raw momentum or raw volume because it requires both price direction and participation.

research_memory.negative_findings: The 2026-05-20 compact-core incremental test did not justify adding this factor to the compact core. Adding it to the four-factor baseline reduced full_base return from 42.92% to 35.58% and full high-cost return from 36.39% to 29.43%. It improved year_2024_base only from -5.96% to -5.18%, worsened January 2024, and left sell-pressure absorption as the largest contribution feature in all 11,033 observed 2024 bars.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Keep as candidate but do not add to the compact-core baseline. Retry only after a sell-pressure contribution cap, health-gated overlay, or execution policy change shows better January/June 2024 robustness without diluting full-window and high-cost returns versus the four-factor compact core.

entries.description: Current 5-minute open-to-previous-close gap.
entries.hypothesis: Short interval gaps capture discrete repricing and temporary order-book imbalance; direction may differ across market regimes, so admission should evaluate both continuation and reversal use.

research_memory.negative_findings: The intraday gap factor was rejected: rank IC was effectively zero, t-stat and hit-rate gates failed, turnover was high, and cost-adjusted spread was negative.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only as a redesigned event feature with overnight/session boundary separation, limit-event conditioning, or explicit reversal buckets.

entries.description: Rolling 48-bar correlation between 5-minute close returns and turnover.
entries.hypothesis: Positive return-turnover correlation indicates participation arriving on up moves rather than indiscriminate turnover spikes, which may be more durable than standalone turnover ratios.

research_memory.negative_findings: Return-turnover correlation worked only in inverted direction, had two stable annual IC slices, and failed the cost-adjusted spread gate in the standard run.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only after decomposing turnover participation into regime or liquidity buckets with positive after-cost spread in annual slices.

entries.description: Rolling 48-bar share of 5-minute close-to-close returns that are negative.
entries.hypothesis: A high fraction of recent negative 5-minute returns indicates persistent intraday selling pressure and weak tape quality. Unlike downside volatility or skewness, the transform intentionally ignores return magnitude and turnover so it can test whether loss persistence alone adds a cleaner risk penalty.
entries.notes: Pre-development research-memory check produced only watchlist warnings, chiefly intraday_return_skewness_5m_w48 and intraday_risk_adjusted_momentum_5m_w48. The material difference is that this factor uses only the frequency of negative bars, not return magnitude, skewness, volatility scaling, or turnover participation. As a gross-exposure overlay, it improved the standard promoted policy only when combined with the downside-volatility gate, raising full-window return from 38.77% to 40.73% and high-cost return from 27.31% to 29.19%, but with worse full-window drawdown. A policy drawdown brake evaluated on week streaming chunks improved the combined-gate full-window result to 41.75% return and -6.49% max drawdown, while day chunks were too slow for practical standard validation.

research_memory.negative_findings: The factor passed hard statistical gates with full coverage, strong inverted rank IC, stable direction in 2023-2025, and moderate top-N turnover, but failed the economic gate because inverted top-minus-bottom spread was negative after the 13 bps cost proxy. A lagged standalone risk gate improved the promoted policy versus the ungated baseline but underperformed the existing downside-volatility gate. The combined negative-return-persistence plus downside-volatility gate passed validation and improved full-window and high-cost returns, but worsened drawdown versus the downside-volatility gate alone. A stricter drawdown-guard parameterization passed validation but reduced full-window return to 39.26%, high-cost return to 27.88%, and worsened max drawdown to -8.31%. The existing score-health budget-deadband regime gate passed validation but reproduced the ungated promoted baseline, so it did not replace the factor gate or improve drawdown. A policy-native monthly-chunk drawdown brake interface was added and validated, but the -7% to 0.5x configuration initially reproduced the v1 gate path because the relevant drawdown happens within a streaming chunk. The final rebalance-level fix preserved the -6.49% drawdown improvement with lower operational complexity than day chunking, while day chunks remained too slow for routine validation.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only with a rebalance-level drawdown brake or liquidity/execution condition. Do not rerun simple negative-return-persistence quantile-threshold tightening, the existing budget-deadband regime schedule, or day-chunk validation, and do not promote as standalone alpha without a positive cost-adjusted spread.

entries.description: Rolling 48-bar downside beta against the cross-sectional median market return.
entries.hypothesis: Names that are more exposed to market-wide downside bars should be penalized in a long-only book because they amplify losses during weak tape regimes even when their own volatility is not extreme.
entries.notes: This factor is designed as a cross-sectional downside-risk penalty rather than a reversal transform. It uses the market-wide median 5m return at each timestamp and measures each name's sensitivity only when the market is down, which is materially different from own-return persistence, volatility, or gap response features. The standard single-factor admission rejected it because rank IC strength, t-stat, directional hit rate, and annual stability were all below threshold despite acceptable coverage and turnover.

research_memory.negative_findings: The factor had full coverage and low turnover, but its absolute rank IC, t-stat, directional hit rate, and stable-year count all failed the admission gate. The yearly profile was mixed with 2024 negative and only one stable year, so the market-downside sensitivity signal was not strong enough to survive the standard alpha screen.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only with a materially different market-state construction, such as sector-relative downside beta or an explicit regime split that conditions on market breadth and limit-up/limit-down pressure.

entries.description: Rolling 48-bar resilience of returns during market limit-pressure states.
entries.hypothesis: Names that hold up better when limit-down opens dominate the tape should be more robust long-only holdings than names that simply track the market beta or own volatility.
entries.notes: This factor is materially different from market beta because it conditions on explicit limit-pressure stress using live-open limit flags. It is not an own-path reversal or volatility transform. The first implementation uses a market-wide pressure proxy computed from the same timestamp's limit-up and limit-down open rates. It should be evaluated as a market-state resilience penalty for long-only selection.

research_memory.negative_findings: The 2026-05-20 legacy revalidation rejected the factor because coverage was only about 21.64%, below the 95% hard admission gate. This is a data coverage failure, not a fresh economic-signal rejection.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only after feature coverage is fixed and the shared admission report passes the 95% coverage gate.

entries.description: Rolling 48-bar resilience of returns during weak market breadth states.
entries.hypothesis: Names that hold up better when the cross-sectional advance-decline ratio is weak should be more robust long-only holdings than names that rely on broad-market participation.
entries.notes: This factor is intentionally different from the rejected market-downside beta family. It uses cross-sectional advance-decline breadth, not median market return, and therefore satisfies the retry condition that called for a materially different market-state construction based on breadth. Standard single-factor admission selected the inverted direction but rejected the factor because directional IC hit rate and stable-year count failed.

research_memory.negative_findings: The factor had full coverage and passed absolute rank IC, t-stat, cost-adjusted spread, and turnover checks only in the inverted direction, but failed the directional IC hit-rate gate at 49.81% and had only one stable annual IC slice. Yearly rank IC signs were mixed: mildly positive in 2023 and 2024, strongly negative in 2025.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only with a materially different breadth construction, such as sector-relative breadth, breadth shock persistence, or an explicit non-bull-market regime split that improves 2023 and 2024 without relying on 2025 inversion.

entries.description: Rolling 48-bar downside turnover absorption ratio.
entries.hypothesis: Names with unusually high turnover per unit downside return may reflect crowded selling or distribution under pressure; lower downside turnover absorption should be cleaner for long-only selection.
entries.notes: This is not a plain turnover ratio or a market beta proxy. It measures downside-only turnover per unit of cumulative negative return over the same rolling window. The standard admission run found a strong inverted direction, suggesting high turnover during downside bars behaves more like crowded distribution than healthy absorption. Standalone decorrelated portfolio validation was positive in full-window, high-cost, and all annual slices, but 2024 max drawdown remained high enough to keep this as a drawdown-watch candidate rather than an immediate promoted factor. A simple -7% drawdown brake with 0.5x exposure reduced full-window drawdown from -31.84% to -20.76%, but lowered full-window return from 60.79% to 33.70% and made 2024 annual return negative, so it is not the preferred risk-control path. A 2024 quick test using downside-volatility state gating improved the 2024 slice from 4.17% return and -29.66% max drawdown to 11.52% return and -21.53% max drawdown. The same downside-volatility gate failed the standard full-window risk-reward check: full_base return fell to 39.69% while max drawdown only improved to -30.27%, versus the standalone 60.79% and -31.84%. Do not continue this gate configuration without a materially different state variable or exposure rule. The compact-core overlay test found that generic contribution caps diluted full-window and high-cost returns, while health shrink left year_2024_base negative and increased sell-pressure contribution concentration. A sell-pressure-specific mild-slow regime guard improved year_2024_base from -5.96% to -1.96%, but full_base fell from 42.92% to 33.52% and full high-cost fell from 36.39% to 27.88%. A narrower combined-rank v2 guard improved year_2024_base to -3.94% with smaller full-window drag, but missed January 2024 and still left 2024 negative. Do not adopt either guard; move next to complementary factor development or a higher-level regime state.

research_memory.negative_findings: The 2026-05-21 compact-core overlay tests did not justify a generic contribution cap, framework-wide health shrink default, or the first targeted mild-slow sell-pressure regime guard. Contribution cap 0.45 improved year_2024_base from -5.96% to -4.21%, but full_base fell from 42.92% to 34.42% and full high-cost fell from 36.39% to 28.85%. Contribution cap 0.50 reduced full_base to 32.05% and year_2024_base to -6.19%. Health shrink preserved full_base at 42.79% and high-cost at 37.14%, but year_2024_base stayed negative at -5.79% and the average largest 2024 contribution share rose from 0.534 to 0.657. The targeted mild-slow regime guard improved year_2024_base to -1.96% and reduced drawdown, but full_base fell to 33.52%, full high-cost fell to 27.88%, and 2025 fell from 31.85% to 23.08%. The narrower combined-rank v2 guard reduced the full-window cost, but year_2024_base remained negative at -3.94% and January 2024 worsened slightly versus baseline.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Do not continue timestamp-level sell-pressure exposure overlays without a materially different state definition. Prefer complementary factor development or a higher-level day/rebalance regime that explicitly protects January and June 2024 without false positives in September and November.

entries.description: Rolling 48-bar decay of downside turnover between earlier and recent half-windows.
entries.hypothesis: Names whose downside turnover has faded across the recent intraday window may reflect seller exhaustion, while names with downside turnover concentrated in the recent half may face intensifying distribution pressure.
entries.notes: This is not a total turnover, signed turnover imbalance, negative-return persistence, sell-pressure absorption, or VWAP-deviation reversal transform. The research-memory check matched the rejected VWAP-deviation factor, but that rejection permits retry under a materially different microstructure hypothesis. This factor satisfies that condition by comparing the earlier and recent halves of the rolling window using only turnover on negative-return bars, so it measures the time profile of downside trading pressure rather than price deviation from a volume-weighted anchor. Standard admission passed in the long direction with full coverage, 0.00688 rank IC, 13.93 t-stat, 53.39% directional hit rate, three stable years, 15.64 bps cost-adjusted spread, and 27.79% top-N turnover. Standard portfolio validation was stopped after full_base evidence because the standalone decorrelated policy returned only 0.73% with -34.29% max drawdown; diagnostics show the long-only top-score basket had negative average forward labels in 2023, 2024, and 2025, so the factor's positive spread came mainly from avoiding even weaker bottom names rather than selecting strong long names.

research_memory.negative_findings: Latest legacy revalidation upgraded single-factor admission to candidate, but high-cost portfolio return remained negative at -1.96%. Treat as cost-fragile and do not promote before a lower-turnover policy fixes the weakness.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only with stricter turnover controls, entry/exit buffers, or as a conditional liquidity-state overlay with positive high-cost validation.

entries.description: Rolling 48-bar price recovery after sell pressure, confirmed by upside turnover participation.
entries.hypothesis: Names that recover more positive return per unit of recent downside return, with recovery confirmed by upside turnover participation, should be better long-only candidates after sell pressure than names that merely avoid the worst downside bucket.
entries.notes: This was explicitly designed as a long-only top-bucket quality signal after the downside-turnover-decay diagnosis. It is not a raw VWAP deviation, reversal transform, sell-pressure absorption ratio, or downside turnover decay. The research-memory check matched the rejected VWAP-deviation factor, but that rejection allows retry with a liquidity-conditioned recovery hypothesis; this factor qualified by using rolling positive-return recovery over downside-return damage and confirming recovery with upside turnover participation. The standard admission run put the factor on watchlist: direction flipped to invert, cost-adjusted spread was -16.56 bps, and the long-only top-bucket health check failed because the highest quantile had negative average forward labels in 2023 and 2024.

research_memory.negative_findings: The recovery transform did not behave as a long-only quality signal. Admission selected the inverted direction, cost-adjusted spread was negative, and the highest quantile had negative average forward labels in 2023 and 2024. Strong apparent sell-pressure recovery likely marks short-horizon exhaustion or overextension rather than a robust next-day long entry.
research_memory.retry_conditions: Retry only with a materially different recovery definition that separates early rebound from late overextension, or after adding an explicit pullback/entry-price condition. Do not rerun this plain recovery ratio as a standalone long-only factor.

entries.description: Multi-dimensional daily moving-average state broadcast onto the 5-minute research grid using only completed prior trading sessions.
entries.hypothesis: Completed daily moving averages capture slower investor anchoring, trend-following pressure, and crowded-trend dispersion that can complement short-horizon intraday liquidity and volatility factors without using same-day unfinished daily bars.
entries.notes: The implementation deliberately shifts daily moving-average features by one completed trading session before merging back to 5-minute bars. Deviation and spread columns express trend/anchor state; slope columns express trend change; ribbon position, dispersion, and trend score summarize cross-window alignment. The research-memory check matched the rejected intraday_range_position_5m_w48 factor, but this candidate is materially different: it uses completed daily moving-average anchors and cross-window MA ribbon geometry, not a 5-minute rolling high-low range location. Admission must evaluate the generated columns individually before any portfolio-level use. Formal promoted-candidate review on 2026-05-19 accepted the fixed high_dispersion_current ribbon-dispersion gross-exposure gate as the leading promoted challenger while keeping score_budget_gate_v1 as the active primary baseline. Evidence: standard validation improved full return from 14.38% to 20.75%, high-cost return from 9.43% to 16.18%, max drawdown from -19.16% to -16.13%, and turnover from 124.78 to 101.86. Walk-forward fixed challenger sum return was 21.22% versus v1 16.91% with 4/4 positive forward windows, but the dynamic train-window selector was rejected. Promotion checks passed monthly concentration, cost stress, and factor attribution gates. Do not add month-specific patches or switch the default strategy without a separate default-change review.

scope: Governance registry for A-share factor discovery, admission, and portfolio validation.
